---
title: "AI-Assisted Development"
abstract: |
  Software development is changing faster than any framework or language ever
  has. AI coding assistants have moved from autocomplete curiosities to agents
  capable of planning, writing, and reviewing entire features autonomously.
  This module guides you how to work *with* that shift rather than around it,
  covering the mental models, tools, and workflows that let developers stay in
  control while letting AI handle the repetitive, mechanical, and
  context-switching parts of the job.
lang: en
draft: true
---

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this module, you will be able to:

- Apply the three stages of AI-assisted development (vibe coding, agentic
  development, spec-driven development) to real projects.
- Configure and use AI coding tools such as GitHub Copilot, MCP servers, and
  agent skills.
- Design effective prompts and specifications that keep you in control of the
  generated output.
- Evaluate when to delegate work to an AI agent and when to write code by hand.

## Session Outline

| # | Session | Topics Covered |
|---|---------|----------------|
| 1 | [Vibe Coding](vibe-coding.qmd) | First stage of AI-assisted development |
| 2 | [Spec-Driven Development](sdd.qmd) | Spec-first workflow, planning, tasks |
| 3 | [Get Started with SDD](sdd-get-started.qmd) | Hands-on Spec Kit setup |
| 4 | [Prompt Engineering](prompt-engineering.qmd) | Designing effective prompts |
| 5 | [Skills](skills.qmd) | Reusable agent skills |
| 6 | [MCP](mcp.qmd) | Model Context Protocol |
| 7 | [GitHub Copilot](copilot.qmd) | Copilot in practice |

## Recommended Literature

<!-- Paper / online-resource profile: Author(s) | Title | Year | Publication Venue -->

| Author(s) | Title | Year | Publication Venue |
|-----------|-------|------|-------------------|
| GitHub | *Improving Token Efficiency in Agentic Workflows* | 2025 | GitHub Blog |
| Anthropic | *Model Context Protocol* | 2024 | anthropic.com |
| GitHub | *GitHub Resources — Articles on AI Development* | 2025 | github.com/resources |

<!--
Spec-Driven Development, the third stage of generative AI assisted development.

1. [vibe-coding](vibe-coding.qmd)
2. [agentic dev]()
3. [sdd](sdd.qmd)
    - [Get Started with Spec Driven Development](get-started-sdd.qmd)

## Tools

### General

- [skills](skills.qmd)
- ACP Agent Client Protocol
- [MCP](mcp.qmd)

### Specific

- [GitHub Copilot](copilot.qmd)
- Claude
- Codex
- Opencode

## Workflow

= self-contained repo or monorepo ?

### Building local AI Agent

- Ollama
- Opencode

## Ressources

- Monorepo permet de bénéficier d’un meilleur contexte pour les agents IA
= solution: git submodule => monorepo

# TODO

- https://github.blog/ai-and-ml/github-copilot/improving-token-efficiency-in-github-agentic-workflows/
- https://github.com/resources/articles
- https://blog.k10s.dev/im-going-back-to-writing-code-by-hand/
-->
